Home › Resources › TDS due dates

TDS payment and return due dates

Last updated: 2026-06-17

TDS deducted is generally deposited by the 7th of the following month. Quarterly TDS returns are generally due on 31 July, 31 October, 31 January, and 31 May.

When must TDS be deposited?

TDS deducted is generally payable by the 7th of the following month, with a separate date for March (generally 30 April) — as per the Income Tax Act, 1961.

Quarterly return due dates

Returns (Form 24Q / 26Q) are generally due as follows: Q1 (April–June) by 31 July; Q2 (July–September) by 31 October; Q3 (October–December) by 31 January; Q4 (January–March) by 31 May.

TDS certificates

Form 16 (salary) is generally issued by 15 June after the financial year; Form 16A (non-salary) is issued quarterly.

Frequently asked questions

When must TDS be deposited?
TDS deducted is generally required to be deposited by the 7th of the following month, with a separate date for March, as per the Income Tax Act, 1961.
What are the TDS return due dates?
Quarterly TDS returns are generally due on 31 July, 31 October, 31 January, and 31 May for Q1 to Q4 respectively.

Source: Income Tax Act, 1961 and CBDT — incometax.gov.in / TRACES. Confirm current dates before filing.
